description:
## Problem

`Link` (blocking.go) only prevents self-loops (`blockerID == blockedID`). It does not detect transitive cycles â€” e.g. A blocks B, B blocks C, C blocks A is silently accepted. The parent hierarchy already rejects cycles (`update.go:46-54`); dependency edges should too.

## Approach

Add a targeted BFS/DFS walk in `Link` before writing the edge. Starting from `blockedID`, follow each issue's `Blocks` list via `readIssue`. If `blockerID` is reachable, return an error like:

```
circular dependency: <blockerID> is already transitively blocked by <blockedID>
```

Use a `visited` set to handle diamond-shaped DAGs efficiently (same pattern as `Tips`). No need to call `LoadEdges` â€” the walk reads only the reachable subgraph.

## Tests

- Direct cycle: A blocks B, then B blocks A â†’ error
- Deep cycle: Aâ†’Bâ†’C, then C blocks A â†’ error
- Diamond (non-cycle): Aâ†’B, Aâ†’C, Bâ†’D, Câ†’D â†’ allowed
- Self-loop: existing test, already rejected

## Notes

- Error message should mention "circular" for consistency with parent cycle errors.
- `Unlink` needs no check (removing edges can't create cycles).
title: Reject cyclic dependency edges in Link
